benchmark_results.query: What are the best museums in Lisbon?
benchmark_results.response:
### 🔵 **Places and Attractions**
🧭 **Result window:** 1-5 of 5.

- **🏛️ Gulbenkian Museum**
    - 📝 **Description:** Closed for renovation until july'26 | Discover the collections, centre of modern art, bookshop, gardens and open-air auditorium where a very important part of Lisbon’s cultural life takes place.
    - 📂 **Category:** Museums
    - 📍 **Address:** [Av. Berna, 45-A, 1067-001, Lisboa](https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Av.+Berna%2C+45-A%2C+1067-001%2C+Lisboa)
    - 💶 **Price:** 20% with Lisboa Card
    - 🎟️ **Tickets:** [Buy tickets](https://gulbenkian.pt/en/useful-information/tickets/)
    - ⭐ **Rating:** TripAdvisor 4.6/5 (8711 reviews)
    - 📞 **Phone:** [+351 217 823 000](tel:+351217823000)
    - ✉️ **Email:** [museu@gulbenkian.pt](mailto:museu@gulbenkian.pt)
    - 🌐 **Website:** [Official website](https://gulbenkian.pt/museu/en/)
    - 🔗 **More details:** [VisitLisboa](https://www.visitlisboa.com/en/places/gulbenkian-museum)

- **🏛️ CAM – Centro de Arte Moderna Gulbenkian**
    - 📝 **Description:** CAM presents an extensive renovation with new exhibition spaces, new galleries and a vast garden area, designed by Japanese architect Kengo Kuma, in collaboration with landscape architect Vladimir Djurovic.
    - 📂 **Category:** Museums & Monuments
    - 📍 **Address:** [Rua Marquês de Fronteira, 2, 1070-295, Lisboa](https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Rua+Marqu%C3%AAs+de+Fronteira%2C+2%2C+1070-295%2C+Lisboa)
    - 💶 **Price:** 20% with Lisboa Card
    - ⭐ **Rating:** TripAdvisor 4/5 (248 reviews)
    - 📞 **Phone:** [+351 217 823 000](tel:+351 217 823 000)
    - ✉️ **Email:** [info@gulbenkian.pt](mailto:info@gulbenkian.pt)
    - 🌐 **Website:** [Official website](https://gulbenkian.pt/cam/en)
    - 🔗 **More details:** [VisitLisboa](https://www.visitlisboa.com/en/places/cam-centro-de-arte-moderna-gulbenkian)

- **🏛️ Maritime Museum**

benchmark_results.query: Tell me about the history of Castelo de São Jorge
benchmark_results.response:
- **📚 Historical Context: Castelo de São Jorge**

- The castle hill has been occupied since ancient periods and became strategically important during Islamic rule in Lisbon.
- The medieval fortification developed as an alcáçova and citadel in the Islamic period, with important references from around the mid-11th century.
- After the conquest of Lisbon in 1147, it became a symbolic seat of royal power and part of the palatial area used by the Portuguese court.
- As court life later shifted toward the riverfront, the castle lost palatial centrality, but its walls, views, and archaeological remains still reveal several layers of Lisbon's urban history.

📌 **Source:** [*Wikipedia/Web*](https://www.wikipedia.org/) | **Updated:** 16:34

benchmark_results.retrieved_context:
[search_history_culture] returned:
📚 **Wikipédia: São Jorge Castle**
🔗 URL: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/S%C3%A3o_Jorge_Castle

São Jorge Castle, sometimes known in English as Saint George's Castle, is a historic castle in the Portuguese capital of Lisbon, located in the freguesia of Santa Maria Maior. Human occupation of the castle hill dates to at least the 8th century BC while the oldest fortifications on the site date from the 2nd century BC. The hill on which Saint George's Castle stands has played an important part in the history of Lisbon, having served as the location of fortifications occupied successively by Phoenicians, Carthaginians, Romans, and Moors, before its conquest by the Portuguese in the 1147 Siege of Lisbon. Since the 12th century, the castle has variously served as a royal palace, a military barracks, home of the Torre do Tombo National Archive, and now as a national monument and museum.

benchmark_results.query: What route does tram 28E take?
benchmark_results.response:
🚋 **Tram 28E Route**

The iconic **Carris Urban** tram 28E operates with two route variants:

**Main Route:**
- Martim Moniz ↔ Campo Ourique (Prazeres)

**Shortened Variant:**
- **Praça Luís de Camões (Bairro Alto)** ↔ **Campo Ourique (Prazeres)**

**Key Stops Along the Route:**
- Martim Moniz
- Praça da Figueira
- Praça do Comércio
- Cais do Sodré
- Santos
- Estrela
- Campo Ourique (Prazeres)

The 28E is one of Lisbon's most famous tram routes, passing through historic neighborhoods including **Alfama**, **Baixa**, **Chiado**, and **Estrela**.

📌 **Source:** [*Carris*](https://www.carris.pt) | **Updated:** 16:50

benchmark_results.query: What are the best museums in Lisbon?
benchmark_results.response:
### 🔵 **Places and Attractions**
🧭 **Result window:** 1-5 of 5.

- **🏛️ Gulbenkian Museum**
    - 📝 **Description:** Closed for renovation until july'26 | Discover the collections, centre of modern art, bookshop, gardens and open-air auditorium where a very important part of Lisbon’s cultural life takes place.
    - 📂 **Category:** Museums
    - 📍 **Address:** [Av. Berna, 45-A, 1067-001, Lisboa](https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Av.+Berna%2C+45-A%2C+1067-001%2C+Lisboa)
    - 💶 **Price:** 20% with Lisboa Card
    - 🎟️ **Tickets:** [Buy tickets](https://gulbenkian.pt/en/useful-information/tickets/)
    - ⭐ **Rating:** TripAdvisor 4.6/5 (8711 reviews)
    - 📞 **Phone:** [+351 217 823 000](tel:+351217823000)
    - ✉️ **Email:** [museu@gulbenkian.pt](mailto:museu@gulbenkian.pt)
    - 🌐 **Website:** [Official website](https://gulbenkian.pt/museu/en/)
    - 🔗 **More details:** [VisitLisboa](https://www.visitlisboa.com/en/places/gulbenkian-museum)

- **🏛️ CAM – Centro de Arte Moderna Gulbenkian**
    - 📝 **Description:** CAM presents an extensive renovation with new exhibition spaces, new galleries and a vast garden area, designed by Japanese architect Kengo Kuma, in collaboration with landscape architect Vladimir Djurovic.
    - 📂 **Category:** Museums & Monuments
    - 📍 **Address:** [Rua Marquês de Fronteira, 2, 1070-295, Lisboa](https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Rua+Marqu%C3%AAs+de+Fronteira%2C+2%2C+1070-295%2C+Lisboa)
    - 💶 **Price:** 20% with Lisboa Card
    - ⭐ **Rating:** TripAdvisor 4/5 (248 reviews)
    - 📞 **Phone:** [+351 217 823 000](tel:+351 217 823 000)
    - ✉️ **Email:** [info@gulbenkian.pt](mailto:info@gulbenkian.pt)
    - 🌐 **Website:** [Official website](https://gulbenkian.pt/cam/en)
    - 🔗 **More details:** [VisitLisboa](https://www.visitlisboa.com/en/places/cam-centro-de-arte-moderna-gulbenkian)

- **🏛️ Maritime Museum**
    - 📝 **Description:** The Maritime Museum is a World of Discoveries. It reflects the importance of the experience and knowledge that the pioneering Portuguese navigators brought to the world.
    - 📂 **Category:** Museums
    - 📍 **Address:** [Praça do Império, 1400-206, Lisboa](https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Pra%C3%A7a+do+Imp%C3%A9rio%2C+1400-206%2C+Lisboa)
    - 🕒 **Hours:** Today: 10:00 - 18:00
    - 💶 **Price:** 20% with Lisboa Card; Children free until age 3; Children (4-12): 4 €; Adult: 8 €; Family: 21 €; Senior: 4 €
    - 🎟️ **Tickets:** [Buy tickets](https://cultura.marinha.pt/pt/museu/planearvisita/bilhetes)
    - ⭐ **Rating:** TripAdvisor 4.3/5 (1368 reviews)
    - 📞 **Phone:** [+351 210 977 388](tel:+351210977388)
    - ✉️ **Email:** [geral.museu@marinha.pt](mailto:geral.museu@marinha.pt)
    - 🌐 **Website:** [Official website](https://cultura.marinha.pt/pt/museumarinha)
    - 🔗 **More details:** [VisitLisboa](https://www.visitlisboa.com/en/places/maritime-museum)

- **🏛️ São Vicente de Fora Museum**
    - 📝 **Description:** Come and discover this unique church and its history, which dates back to the reconquest of Lisbon and the centuries that followed right up to today.
    - 📂 **Category:** Museums
    - 📍 **Address:** [Largo de São Vicente, 1100, Lisboa](https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Largo+de+S%C3%A3o+Vicente%2C+1100%2C+Lisboa)
    - 🕒 **Hours:** Today: 10:00 - 18:00
    - 💶 **Price:** 25% with Lisboa Card
    - 🎟️ **Tickets:** [Buy tickets](https://mosteirodesaovicentedefora.com/en/tickets/)
    - ⭐ **Rating:** TripAdvisor 4.6/5 (734 reviews)
    - 📞 **Phone:** [+351 218 810 559](tel:+351218810559)
    - ✉️ **Email:** [museu@patriarcado-lisboa.pt](mailto:museu@patriarcado-lisboa.pt)
    - 🌐 **Website:** [Official website](http://www.patriarcado-lisboa.pt/)
    - 🔗 **More details:** [VisitLisboa](https://www.visitlisboa.com/en/places/sao-vicente-de-fora-museum)

- **🏛️ National Tile Museum**
    - 📝 **Description:** The museum is set in Madre de Deus Convent, founded in 1509 and its collections allow a journey through the history of tile, from 15th century till present days.
    - 📂 **Category:** Museums & Monuments
    - 📍 **Address:** [Rua da Madre de Deus, 4, 1900-312, Lisboa](https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Rua+da+Madre+de+Deus%2C+4%2C+1900-312%2C+Lisboa)
    - 💶 **Price:** Free with Lisboa Card
    - ⭐ **Rating:** TripAdvisor 4.5/5 (4599 reviews)
    - 📞 **Phone:** [+351 218 100 340](tel:+351218100340)
    - ✉️ **Email:** [geral.mnaz@museusemonumentos.pt](mailto:geral.mnaz@museusemonumentos.pt)
    - 🌐 **Website:** [Official website](https://www.museunacionaldoazulejo.pt/)
    - 🔗 **More details:** [VisitLisboa](https://www.visitlisboa.com/en/places/national-tile-museum)

📌 **Source:** [*VisitLisboa Places*](https://www.visitlisboa.com/en/places) | **Updated:** 17:07

- **🎵 Gipsy Kings by Diego Baliardo | "A Tu Vera” Tour**
    - 📝 **Description:** The Gipsy Kings by Diego Baliardo return to Portugal with the “A Tu Vera” Tour 2026, celebrating 35 years of international hits that have become true anthems of flamenco rumba.
    - 📍 **Address:** [Coliseu de Lisboa, Rua das Portas de Santo Antão, 96, Lisboa, 1150-269](https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Coliseu+de+Lisboa%2C+Rua+das+Portas+de+Santo+Ant%C3%A3o%2C+96%2C+Lisboa%2C+1150-269)
    - 📅 **Date/Time:** 10 May
    - ⏱️ **Duration:** 🎯 Single day
    - 📂 **Category:** Music
    - 💰 **Price:** From €25 to €50
    - 🌐 [More details](https://www.visitlisboa.com/en/events/gipsy-kings-by-diego-baliardo-a-tu-vera-tour-2026)
    - 🎟️ [Tickets](https://www.bol.pt/Comprar/Bilhetes/168176-gipsy_kings_by_diego_baliardo_a_tu_vera_tour_2026-coliseu_de_lisboa/)

- **🎵 Fado in the Garden of the House of Amália Rodrigues**
    - 📝 **Description:** The Secret Garden hosts unique voice and guitar concerts. The acoustic and intimate concerts can also be preceded by a guided tour of the Casa-Museum.
    - 📍 **Address:** [Casa-Museu Amália Rodrigues, Rua de São Bento, 193, 1250-219, Lisboa](https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Casa-Museu+Am%C3%A1lia+Rodrigues%2C+Rua+de+S%C3%A3o+Bento%2C+193%2C+1250-219%2C+Lisboa)
    - 📅 **Date/Time:** 12 May | 28 Apr | 30 Apr | (+78 more dates)

    - 📂 **Category:** Music
    - 💰 **Price:** From €25
    - 🌐 [More details](https://www.visitlisboa.com/en/events/fado-in-the-garden-of-the-house-of-amalia-rodrigues)
    - 🎟️ [Tickets](https://blueticket.meo.pt/en/event/12199/fado-no-jardim-da-casa-de-amalia-rodrigues)

- **🎵 Fado in Chiado**
    - 📝 **Description:** Fado in Chiado is a live Fado Show an excellent way to discover culture and the city.
    - 📍 **Address:** [Cine Theatro Gymnásio - Espaço Chiado, Rua da Misericórdia 14,, 1200-094, Lisboa](https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Cine+Theatro+Gymn%C3%A1sio+-+Espa%C3%A7o+Chiado%2C+Rua+da+Miseric%C3%B3rdia+14%2C%2C+1200-094%2C+Lisboa)
    - 📅 **Date/Time:** 11 May | 09 May | 12 May | (+304 more dates)

    - 📂 **Category:** Music
    - 💰 **Price:** Paid
    - 🕐 **Schedule:** From Monday to Saturday • 17.30 and 19:00 • 50 minutes duration

benchmark_results.query: Tell me about the history of Castelo de São Jorge
benchmark_results.response:
- **📚 Historical Context: Castelo de São Jorge**

- The castle hill has been occupied since ancient periods and became strategically important during Islamic rule in Lisbon.
- The medieval fortification developed as an alcáçova and citadel in the Islamic period, with important references from around the mid-11th century.
- After the conquest of Lisbon in 1147, it became a symbolic seat of royal power and part of the palatial area used by the Portuguese court.
- As court life later shifted toward the riverfront, the castle lost palatial centrality, but its walls, views, and archaeological remains still reveal several layers of Lisbon's urban history.

📌 **Source:** [*Wikipedia/Web*](https://www.wikipedia.org/) | **Updated:** 17:09

benchmark_results.retrieved_context:
[search_history_culture] returned:
📚 **Wikipédia: São Jorge Castle**
🔗 URL: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/S%C3%A3o_Jorge_Castle

São Jorge Castle, sometimes known in English as Saint George's Castle, is a historic castle in the Portuguese capital of Lisbon, located in the freguesia of Santa Maria Maior. Human occupation of the castle hill dates to at least the 8th century BC while the oldest fortifications on the site date from the 2nd century BC. The hill on which Saint George's Castle stands has played an important part in the history of Lisbon, having served as the location of fortifications occupied successively by Phoenicians, Carthaginians, Romans, and Moors, before its conquest by the Portuguese in the 1147 Siege of Lisbon. Since the 12th century, the castle has variously served as a royal palace, a military barracks, home of the Torre do Tombo National Archive, and now as a national monument and museum.

benchmark_results.query: I'd like a few quiet lesser-known places in Lisbon.
benchmark_results.expected_behavior: Recommend real, verified places only and explicitly avoid guessing when data is limited.
benchmark_results.response:
Here are some quiet, lesser-known places in Lisbon that offer a peaceful escape from the crowds:

- **🏛️ Galeria Subterrânea do Loreto**
    - 📝 **Description:** A hidden underground gallery beneath the streets of Lisbon, offering a fascinating glimpse into the city's subterranean water infrastructure. A truly unique and quiet experience away from the tourist areas.
    - 📂 **Category:** Monuments
    - 📍 **Address:** [Praça do Príncipe Real, 1250-096, Lisboa](https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Pra%C3%A7a+do+Pr%C3%ADncipe+Real%2C+1250-096%2C+Lisboa)
    - ⭐ **Rating:** TripAdvisor 4.2/5 (201 reviews)
    - 📞 **Phone:** [+351 218 100 215](tel:+351218100215)
    - 🌐 **Website:** [Official website](http://www.museudaagua.blogspot.pt)

- **🏛️ Water Museum - Barbadinhos Steam Pumping Station**
    - 📝 **Description:** A collection of 18th and 19th-century monuments and buildings that tell the story of Lisbon's water supply history. This industrial heritage site is rarely crowded and offers a peaceful, educational visit.
    - 📂 **Category:** Monuments
    - 📍 **Address:** [Rua do Alviela, 12, 1170-012, Lisboa](https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Rua+do+Alviela%2C+12%2C+1170-012%2C+Lisboa)
    - 💶 **Price:** 50% with Lisboa Card
    - ⭐ **Rating:** TripAdvisor 3.8/5 (62 reviews)
    - 📞 **Phone:** [+351 218 100 215](tel:+351218100215)
    - 🌐 **Website:** [Official website](http://www.epal.pt)

- **🏛️ Estufa Fria**
    - 📝 **Description:** A lush greenhouse garden within Parque Eduardo VII, filled with exotic plants and tranquil pathways. It's a serene botanical oasis in the heart of the city, perfect for quiet contemplation.
    - 📂 **Category:** Museums & Monuments
    - 📍 **Address:** [Parque Eduardo VII, 1070-099, Lisboa](https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Parque+Eduardo+VII%2C+1070-099%2C+Lisboa)
    - 💶 **Price:** Free with Lisboa Card
    - ⭐ **Rating:** TripAdvisor 4.4/5 (632 reviews)
    - 📞 **Phone:** [+351 218 170 996](tel:+351218170996)
    - 🌐 **Website:** [Official website](https://informacoeseservicos.lisboa.pt/contactos/diretorio-da-cidade/estufa-fria-de-lisboa)

- **🏛️ Aqueducts and Mãe d'Água**
    - 📝 **Description:** An impressive 18th-century aqueduct system with the Mãe d'Água reservoir. The surrounding area near Praça das Amoreiras is peaceful, and you can combine your visit with the nearby Arpad Szenes-Vieira da Silva Foundation and its quiet garden.
    - 📂 **Category:** Monuments
    - 📍 **Address:** [Praça das Amoreiras, nº 10, Lisboa](https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Pra%C3%A7a+das+Amoreiras%2C+n%C2%BA+10%2C+Lisboa)
    - ⭐ **Rating:** TripAdvisor 4.4/5 (171 reviews)
    - 🌐 **Website:** [Official website](http://museudaagua.blogspot.pt)

- **🏛️ Torre da Igreja do Castelo de São Jorge**
    - 📝 **Description:** While São Jorge Castle itself is popular, this church bell tower offers a more intimate and less crowded viewpoint with stunning historic scenery over the city. A hidden gem within the castle neighborhood.
    - 📂 **Category:** Museums & Monuments
    - 📍 **Address:** [Largo de Santa Cruz do Castelo, 15, 1100-060, Lisboa](https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Largo+de+Santa+Cruz+do+Castelo%2C+15%2C+1100-060%2C+Lisboa)
    - 💶 **Price:** 20% with Lisboa Card
    - ⭐ **Rating:** TripAdvisor 3.9/5 (109 reviews)
    - 📞 **Phone:** [+351 215 806 032](tel:+351215806032)
    - 🌐 **Website:** [Official website](https://igrejadocastelo.pt/)

benchmark_results.query: Quais são as melhores praias perto de Lisboa?
benchmark_results.response:
Aqui estão as melhores praias perto de Lisboa, de acordo com os dados disponíveis:

### 🏖️ Praias da Costa da Caparica

- 📝 **Descrição:** Conjunto de praias de surf muito populares, localizadas do outro lado do rio Tejo, acessíveis de ferry e autocarro desde Lisboa.
- 📂 **Categoria:** Spots de Surf
- 📍 **Morada:** [Costa da Caparica](https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Costa+da+Caparica)
- ⭐ **Avaliação:** 4.2/5 (571 avaliações)
- 🔗 **Mais detalhes:** [VisitLisboa](https://www.visitlisboa.com/en/places/beaches-of-costa-da-caparica)

### 🏖️ Praias da Ericeira

- 📝 **Descrição:** Reserva Mundial de Surf, com várias praias de excelência para surfistas de todos os níveis. Ambiente pitoresco de vila piscatória.
- 📂 **Categoria:** Reserva Nacional de Surf
- 📍 **Morada:** [Ericeira | Mafra](https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Ericeira+%7C+Mafra)
- ⭐ **Avaliação:** 4.2/5 (261 avaliações)
- 🌐 **Website:** [Website oficial](https://www.cm-mafra.pt/pages/529)
- 🔗 **Mais detalhes:** [VisitLisboa](https://www.visitlisboa.com/en/places/beaches-of-ericeira)

### 🏖️ Praia D'el Rey Golf & Beach Resort

- 📝 **Descrição:** Resort de praia e golfe com acesso privilegiado à costa atlântica, situado na região de Óbidos.
- 📂 **Categoria:** Golfe com Vista Mar
- 📍 **Morada:** [Praia D'el Rey, Vale das Janelas, 2510-451, Óbidos](https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Praia+D%27el+Rey%2C+Vale+das+Janelas%2C+2510-451%2C+%C3%93bidos)
- 📞 **Telefone:** [+351 262 905 005](tel:+351262905005)
- ⭐ **Avaliação:** 4.4/5 (1796 avaliações)
- 🌐 **Website:** [Website oficial](http://www.praia-del-rey.com/index.php/golf/)
- 🔗 **Mais detalhes:** [VisitLisboa](https://www.visitlisboa.com/en/places/praia-d-el-rey-golf-beach-resort)
- Dica: A Costa da Caparica é a opção mais acessível a partir de Lisboa (cerca de 20-30 minutos de transportes). A Ericeira é ideal para surf e ambiente de vila piscatória (cerca de 40 minutos de carro). A Praia D'el Rey é perfeita para quem procura um resort de luxo com praia privada.

📌 **Fonte:** [*VisitLisboa Locais*](https://www.visitlisboa.com/pt-pt/locais) | **Atualizado:** 17:12

## Cervejaria Ramiro Restaurant

I was looking forward to eating at a lot of restaurants during my trip to Lisbon, Portugal.

But nothing got me more excited than thinking about Cervejaria Ramiro Restaurant, known to serve some of Lisbon’s best seafood. [...] ## Conclusion

Some say the seas off of Portugal are home to the best seafood in the world. So it goes without saying that you’ll eat a lot of amazing seafood when you’re in Lisbon, and there are plenty of good restaurants to choose from.

Cervejaria Ramiro is famous for both locals and tourists because they serve honest high quality, fresh, and extraordinary seafood at fair and reasonable prices, with friendly service. That’s the formula that works, and which has made them one of the best seafood restaurants in Lisbon.

The seafood was extraordinary, and the entire meal experience, made it one of the best seafood feasts I’ve ever had!

# Cervejaria Ramiro

4.47 Votes   Currently Open

 Address:  Av. Almirante Reis nº1 - H, 1150-007 Lisboa, Portugal, Lisbon Map
 Timings: 12:00 pm - 11:59 pm  Details
 Phone: +351-218851024
 Tags: Restaurant

Tickets From $

Book Now [...] Tickets From $

Book Now

Cervejaria Ramiro is an informal and relaxed seafood restaurant that was established somewhere in the 1950’s and since then has been attracting customers with its lip-smacking food items. Today, the restaurant is recognized for the excellent quality of raw materials that is used for the preparation of food and its services. From clams to tiger shrimps, goose barnacles to crawfish, oysters to prego, this seafood paradise truly has an extensive menu. You can opt for beer, white wine and other drinks to compliment the seafood. The staff members here at Cervejaria Ramiro are rather friendly and would help you in making choices in case you feel overwhelmed with the choices.

## How to Reach Cervejaria Ramiro

 Buses 28E, 734 to stop - Igreja dos Anjos

## Get The Wonderlust Newsletter

The world at your feet:  
 New destinations and experiences, and the best travel deals!

Subscribe, It's Free! [...] I have a thing about standing in line for a table at a restaurant. I won’t do it. I refuse to do it. There is no food in Western Civilization worth standing in line for. Not even donuts. On occasion, however, I will make a mistake. Here’s how that works: you’re in Lisbon on a Saturday, around noon o’clock, severely hungover because you drank a lot of porto the night before, or probably drank a lot of porto — it’s the national drink of Portugal and you are nothing if not scrupulous in your adherence to local norms. Anyway, you drank a lot of something. You roll out of bed looking for food as a balm for your pickled gut. There’s a modest-looking seafood restaurant right around the corner — like fifty yards from the door of the place where you’re staying. You’ve been traveling around Europe [...] Turns out that Cervejaria Ramiro is famous for its lobsters. Anthony Bourdain featured the place on one of his television shows. People will line up not just for hours but for days to be seated at this lobster palace. (I may be over-selling the lobsters here; better provide a picture of the one I ate…)

CERVEJARIA RAMIRO

That is one hell of a lobster Jim Greer

In fact I’d lucked out. I’d all unknowing stumbled through the door of what is possibly Lisbon’s finest eatery just before the brunch rush. Or brush.
